skill.name: code-review
skill.description: Guides authors, reviewers, and teams through practical pull request review workflows, checklists, and comment classification.
skill.summary: Improve pull request quality with structured review workflows and templates.
skill.author: Claude Assistant

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, network, and security-keyword hits, but review shows they are documentation examples and templates. The main residual risk is legitimate project-local command guidance, which can execute repository scripts when a user asks the assistant to run checks. No evidence found of malicious intent, prompt injection, credential exfiltration, hidden scripts, or unauthorized network behavior.

medium_findings.title: Project-Local Command Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends running test, lint, type-check, git, and custom review commands as part of normal code review. This is legitimate for a coding workflow, but project-defined scripts can execute arbitrary repository code if the user authorizes execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command guidance is explicit and repeated, so the operational risk is real. The context is normal review automation rather than malicious execution, which keeps confidence below very high.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Findings Are Template References
low_findings.description: The filesystem hits point to CODEOWNERS paths, environment-example filenames, and relative documentation links. These are review templates and references, not hidden file reads or path traversal behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible markdown templates and relative links. No file-reading code or traversal logic is present in the reviewed context.

low_findings.title: Security Keyword Hits Are Review Examples
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto, SQL injection, and credential-related static hits are examples of issues reviewers should detect. They do not implement vulnerable code or request access to secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The terms appear in educational examples and checklist text. I found no executable code path using weak cryptography or handling credentials.

low_findings.title: Single Network Finding Is Documentation Link
low_findings.description: The only network risk factor is a GitHub documentation URL inside a CODEOWNERS template. It is a reference link and does not cause outbound requests by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a static markdown URL to official GitHub documentation. There is no fetch call, webhook, or data transmission behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell-Like Commands In Markdown Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: Markdown examples include npm, git, and slash-command workflows. These are intended review steps, but users should approve execution and understand project scripts before running them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is present in multiple user-facing examples. The surrounding text frames it as optional quality checks, not covert command execution.

content.value_statement: Unclear reviews slow teams and let defects reach production. This skill provides structured workflows, templates, and comment labels for faster pull request reviews.
actual_capabilities: Guides authors through pre-PR checks for tests, linting, scope, and descriptions.
Guides reviewers through a two-pass process for design and implementation review.
Provides comment labels for critical issues, issues, suggestions, nits, questions, and praise.
Includes PR, CODEOWNERS, author checklist, and reviewer checklist templates.
Offers team adoption guidance with rollout phases, metrics, and review SLAs.
limitations: It provides markdown guidance and templates, not a complete review automation tool.
Slash commands mentioned in examples may require separate tools or local setup.
It cannot guarantee that tests, linting, or security checks pass without running them.
Teams must adapt templates to their repository structure and ownership model.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Pull Request
use_cases.description: Use the author checklist to validate tests, scope, documentation, deployment notes, and review readiness before opening a PR.

use_cases.title: Review a Complex Change
use_cases.description: Use the two-pass method to review architecture first, then correctness, testing, readability, and documentation.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Reviews
use_cases.description: Adopt shared PR templates, CODEOWNERS guidance, comment labels, review SLAs, and metrics across an engineering team.

prompt_templates.title: Run Author Checklist
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner author preparing a small PR
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the code-review skill to check whether my pull request is ready. Focus on tests, scope, description, and deployment notes.

prompt_templates.title: Classify Review Feedback
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me classify these review comments as critical, issue, suggestion, nit, question, or praise. Explain which items block merge.

prompt_templates.title: Perform Two-Pass Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Reviewer assigned to a medium PR
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through a two-pass review. Start with design, security, and scope, then move to correctness, tests, and readability.

prompt_templates.title: Create Team Review Process
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a code review rollout plan for my team. Include templates, CODEOWNERS guidance, SLAs, metrics, and adoption phases.

output_examples.input: I need to open a PR for an authentication change.
output_examples.output: The skill returns a readiness checklist covering tests, linting, security notes, rollback planning, PR size, and reviewer assignment.

output_examples.input: I am reviewing a 350-line backend PR.
output_examples.output: The skill guides a first pass for architecture and security, then a second pass for logic, tests, errors, and maintainability.

output_examples.input: Our reviews are slow and inconsistent.
output_examples.output: The skill proposes a phased rollout with PR templates, comment labels, CODEOWNERS ownership, response targets, and review metrics.

best_practices: Keep PRs small, focused, and backed by a clear test plan.
Review design and risk before spending time on line-level details.
Label comments by severity so authors know what blocks merge.
anti_patterns: Opening large PRs without context, self-review, or test evidence.
Blocking merges on style nits that should be automated by tooling.
Assigning too many reviewers without clear ownership or response expectations.

faq.question: Does this skill review code automatically?
faq.answer: It guides review workflows and templates. Automated checks depend on your local tools and any connected review commands.

faq.question: Can it create a PR template?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a pull request template that teams can adapt for GitHub or GitLab workflows.

faq.question: Does it require Claude Code?
faq.answer: No. The guidance is useful in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code, but some slash-command examples may be Claude Code specific.

faq.question: What makes the two-pass review useful?
faq.answer: It checks design, scope, and risk before detailed implementation review. This reduces wasted effort on flawed approaches.

faq.question: How should teams handle comment labels?
faq.answer: Use labels consistently. Critical and issue comments block merge, while suggestions, nits, questions, and praise communicate intent.

faq.question: Is this safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: The skill is documentation-focused. Users should still approve any local command execution and avoid sharing private code externally.
